data.title: BNB Surpasses 630 USDT with a Narrowed 1.54% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 07, 2026, 09:48 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 630 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 630.01001 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.54% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Dubai airport suspended flights due to explosions; Air China flight from Beijing to Dubai was unable to land and circled in the air.
data.description: An explosion was heard at Dubai International Airport in the United Arab Emirates this morning, prompting some passengers to be evacuated to the basement level for safety. The UAE announced the suspension of all flights to and from Dubai. It has been learned that Air China's first resumed flight from Beijing to Dubai, originally scheduled to land at Dubai International Airport an hour ago, is currently still circling over Oman. (CBN)

data.title: XRP's Potential Surge to $100: Analyzing Market Impact
data.description:
Entrepreneur Patrick Bet-David has proposed a model suggesting that XRP could reach a value of $100 if it manages to process approximately 5% of SWIFT's reported $5 trillion in daily transactions. According to NS3.AI, Bet-David highlights that SWIFT handles around $1.25 quadrillion annually, providing a context for the potential market size XRP could tap into.

The article further mentions the conclusion of the SEC lawsuit concerning XRP, which ended following a ruling in 2023 and the case closure in August 2025. This development is seen as a significant factor in XRP's market dynamics, potentially influencing its future valuation.

data.title: South Korean financial authorities plan to ban companies from investing in stablecoins.
data.description: The South Korean Financial Services Commission is currently drafting "Guidelines for Corporate Virtual Currency Trading," which will exclude stablecoins from the scope of investment permits. These guidelines aim to allow listed corporations and professional investment registered corporations to conduct digital asset trading for investment or financial purposes. To prevent disorderly investment in the early stages of the market, the authorities have decided to exclude USDT, USDC, and other USD-denominated stablecoins from the permitted scope. One reason for this exclusion is that the current South Korean Foreign Exchange Transaction Law does not recognize stablecoins as a means of external payment. Including stablecoins in the investment permit scope would contradict the existing legal system, effectively allowing companies to use stablecoins for trade and other commercial purposes. The South Korean National Assembly is currently reviewing an amendment to the Foreign Exchange Transaction Law, which proposes to recognize stablecoins as a means of payment; this bill was proposed last October. It is understood that some listed companies with a high proportion of trade have requested that stablecoins be included in the permitted scope so that they can use stablecoins for foreign exchange hedging. Even if excluded from the guidelines, companies can still trade stablecoins through personal wallets or overseas exchanges. Industry insiders revealed that the relevant working group has completed its work, but the release time of the guidelines is linked to the legislative process of the Basic Law on Digital Assets.

data.description:
Kalshi is facing a class action lawsuit over how it resolved a prediction market on Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

data.title: Mediterranean Shipping Company Imposes Emergency Fuel Surcharge
data.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) has announced the implementation of an emergency fuel surcharge on all cargo transported from the Mediterranean and Black Sea regions to the Indian subcontinent, the Red Sea, and East Africa. According to Odaily, this surcharge will be effective from March 16 until further notice. The surcharge is set at $60 per standard container and $90 per refrigerated container.

data.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) will impose an emergency fuel surcharge on all cargo shipped from the Mediterranean and Black Sea regions to the Indian subcontinent, the Red Sea, and East Africa. MSC: From March 16th until further notice, the emergency fuel surcharge for cargo on the Mediterranean and Black Sea to East Africa route will be adjusted to US$60 per TEU and US$90 per refrigerated container. (Jinshi)

data.title: Software Sector Faces Selloff Challenges Impacting OpenAI
data.description:
The software industry is experiencing a significant selloff, posing challenges for companies like OpenAI. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns about the sector's performance and its implications for major players. The downturn in software stocks has raised questions about the sustainability of growth for firms heavily reliant on artificial intelligence and other advanced technologies.

OpenAI, known for its cutting-edge AI developments, is among the companies affected by the market's volatility. The selloff reflects broader uncertainties in the tech industry, as investors reassess the value and potential of software companies amid changing economic conditions.

Market analysts are closely monitoring the situation, noting that the selloff could lead to strategic shifts within affected companies. The focus is on how these firms will adapt to the evolving landscape and maintain their competitive edge in a challenging environment.

The software sector's performance is crucial for the overall tech industry, influencing investment decisions and future growth prospects. As companies navigate these turbulent times, the emphasis is on innovation and resilience to overcome the current challenges.

data.title: Crypto Founder Says Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP Suffer Quantum Threat Risks, but XRP Has an Edge
data.description:
A prominent crypto founder says Bitcoin, Ethereum, and XRP all suffer quantum threat risks, but XRP may have an advantage over the others. Discussions about the impact of quantum computing on blockchain security have picked up the pace as researchers assess whether future quantum machines could break the cryptographic systems used by major networks. Visit Website
source: https://thecryptobasic.com/2026/03/07/crypto-founder-says-bitcoin-ethereum-xrp-suffer-quantum-threat-risks-but-xrp-has-an-edge/

data.title: The Israeli military claims it launched airstrikes on Iranian missile storage facilities with dozens of warplanes.
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) issued a statement saying that the IDF launched a new round of airstrikes against Tehran and central Iran, deploying more than 80 warplanes to attack multiple military targets, including missile storage facilities. The statement said the airstrikes were "intended to bolster Iran's missile launch and defense capabilities." Targets included an underground facility used for storing ballistic missiles, containing military bunkers and a command center. Hundreds of military personnel were present at the time of the attack. The IDF also attacked a storage facility of Iranian missile forces containing military bunkers and launch devices, as well as missile launch sites in several regions of Iran. The IDF also attacked Imam Hussein University in Iran. The IDF stated that the university is the main military academy of the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) and houses "various military facilities" of the IRGC. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran's General Staff: Strikes against the US and Israel will continue.
data.description: A spokesperson for the General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces stated that strikes against the United States and Israel will continue. The spokesperson said that Iran has so far struck all bases that have been the source of aggression against Iran; those countries that have not provided space and facilities to the United States and Israel are not Iran's targets and will not be targets in the future. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran Continues Strikes Against U.S. and Israel
data.description: Iran's armed forces spokesperson announced that the country will persist in its strikes against the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, the spokesperson stated that Iran has targeted all bases identified as sources of aggression against the nation. Countries that do not provide space and facilities to the U.S. and Israel are not and will not be targets of Iran's actions.

data.title: Cardano's Selling Pressure Decreases by Nearly 50%
data.description: On-chain data indicates a significant reduction in Cardano's selling pressure, with the Spent Coins Age Band metric decreasing from approximately 171.42 million to 89.97 million coins. According to NS3.AI, this decline, coupled with a bullish divergence on the 12-hour RSI and a local profitability bottom, may facilitate a rebound attempt over the weekend. ADA is currently trading near a support level of $0.255–$0.258, while resistance zones are identified between $0.270 and $0.294 as potential targets for upward movement.

data.title: Kurds Considered for Role in Iran Regime Change Efforts
data.description: The Kurds are being evaluated as a potential ground force to facilitate regime change in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, drawing parallels to the situation in Libya in 2011, which raises concerns about the potential outcomes of such an approach. The strategy involves leveraging the Kurds' position and capabilities to influence political shifts in Iran, reminiscent of past international interventions. The implications of this strategy are being closely analyzed, considering historical precedents and regional dynamics.

data.title: Iran Reports Extensive Damage to Civilian Infrastructure Amid Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran's Red Crescent Society has reported significant damage to civilian infrastructure across the country following attacks by the United States and Israel. According to Jin10, the organization's chairman stated that a total of 6,668 civilian facilities have been affected. This development highlights the ongoing tensions and the impact on Iran's civilian areas. The situation continues to evolve as geopolitical dynamics play out in the region.
